_comment: DishtaYantra v1.5.1 - Example DAG demonstrating Non-Dictionary Message Packaging
_description: This DAG shows how to use auto_package_non_dict to handle CSV strings, JSON arrays, and other non-dict messages from Kafka and other sources
_note: This is an EXAMPLE DAG in config/example/dags/ - copy to config/dags/ to use. Requires Kafka running on localhost:9092
name: message_packaging_demo

subscribers._comment: Kafka subscriber receiving CSV trade strings like 'TRADE_001,BUY,100,AAPL,150.25'
subscribers.name: kafka_csv_trades

config._explanation: With auto_package_non_dict=true, a CSV string like 'TRADE_001,BUY,100,AAPL' becomes: {'payload': 'TRADE_001,BUY,100,AAPL', '_packaged': true, '_original_type': 'str', '_metadata': {...}}

subscribers._comment: Kafka subscriber for JSON arrays - uses different wrapper key
subscribers.name: kafka_json_batches

config._explanation: JSON arrays are packaged with wrapper key 'items': {'items': [{'id': 1}, {'id': 2}], '_packaged': true, '_original_type': 'list'}

subscribers._comment: Kafka subscriber with packaging DISABLED - for when Kafka sends dict messages
subscribers.name: kafka_dict_input

config._explanation: Set to false when Kafka messages are already JSON dictionaries - no packaging needed

subscribers._comment: In-memory subscriber with priority queue and packaging (no metadata for performance)
subscribers.name: inmemory_market_data

config._explanation: Metadata disabled for high-frequency feeds to reduce overhead. Uses inmemory:// URI format (v1.5.1)

config._note: This calculator receives packaged messages. Check data.get('_packaged') to detect them.

nodes._comment: Entry node for CSV trade strings from Kafka
nodes.name: csv_input_node

_usage_notes.description: This DAG demonstrates the v1.5.1 non-dictionary message packaging feature with Kafka
prerequisites: Create topics: csv_trades, batch_data, json_records, processed_trades

key_configurations.auto_package_non_dict: Enable automatic wrapping of non-dict messages (default: true)
key_configurations.package_wrapper_key: Key name for original data in wrapped dict (default: 'payload')
key_configurations.preserve_raw_on_error: Keep raw data if packaging fails (default: true)
